project.name: Headhunt Manager (FDIWork)
project.type: Next.js 16 App Router, Prisma ORM, PostgreSQL, TailwindCSS
project.status: Sprint 1-3 complete, Sprint 5 complete, production deployed on Vercel + Neon, Sprint 4 pending real-user onboarding

features: Authentication via NextAuth for CRM and custom JWT for employer portal
Candidate management with tags, seniority, skills, reminders, notes, multi-CV, languages, work history, trash/restore
Client management with contacts, revenue summary, paginated select, and client import
Job management with kanban pipeline, smart assign modal, job bridge card, and FDIWork sync
Dashboard with pipeline overview, recent activity, reminders, deadline alerts, and revenue widgets
FDIWork public website with jobs, companies, apply flow, and employer portal
Admin moderation with application import to CRM, employer linking, packages, and employer management
Global search, notification surfaces, bulk candidate actions, dark mode, and responsive dashboard UX
Excel/CSV import for candidates and clients with preview, validation, dedup, and error reporting
Production hardening with Redis rate limit, file signature validation, Sentry optional init, Neon pooled Postgres support

database_schema.summary: ATS CRM + employer portal + public job board + ops/audit tracking
database_schema.relationships_explained: CRM User owns Candidates, Clients, and Jobs. JobOrder links Client, assigned recruiter, and JobCandidate pipeline rows. Employer optionally links to a CRM Client and owns public JobPostings. Applications can be imported into CRM Candidate and JobCandidate pipeline. ActivityLog stores mutation history for stage, status, notes, and imports.
notable_constraints: JobPosting.skills is String[] and JobOrder.requiredSkills is String[]
Employer unique email and optional unique clientId
Candidate import dedups by email/phone; client import dedups by companyName/website

conventions: Server Actions return success/error objects and user-facing errors stay in Vietnamese
Data access should go through data-layer modules instead of direct Prisma in actions
Dashboard and shared UI use semantic design tokens instead of hardcoded gray/white classes
Neon is the canonical Postgres provider; prefer pooled URL in production
Import flows must preview, validate, and report row-level errors before mutating data
Candidate primary CV remains mirrored to legacy Candidate.cvFileUrl/cvFileName for compatibility
Ownership scope uses ViewerScope and filters by createdById/assignedToId for MEMBER users
security_notes: Employer JWT TTL is 1 day and employer must remain ACTIVE
Public apply, candidate CV upload, and avatar upload validate file signatures by magic bytes
Upstash Redis rate limit is wired with dev fallback when env is missing
Sentry initializes only when SENTRY_DSN is configured
Avatar upload enforces ownership checks to prevent IDOR
infra_notes: Prisma client uses @prisma/adapter-pg with pg Pool and pooled connection preference
postinstall runs prisma generate to keep Vercel production builds in sync with schema
There is still a pg sslmode warning recommending verify-full instead of require
known_issues_fixed: Candidate skills and JobOrder.requiredSkills normalized and indexed with GIN
CV replace flow changed from delete-before-upload to upload-update-delete to avoid race condition
Tag update path wrapped in Prisma transaction
Parser header normalization now handles Vietnamese Đ/đ so import maps phone/address columns correctly
